records.ety: [For older garth or garse, OF. garser to scarify, F. gercer to chap, perh. from an assumed LL. carptiare, fr. L. carpere, carptum, to pluck, separate into parts; cf. LL. carptare to wound. Cf. Carpet.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Gashed (gsht); p. pr. & vb. n. Gashing.]

defs: To make a gash, or long, deep incision in; -- applied chiefly to incisions in flesh.
quotes: Grievously gashed or gored to death.

defs: A deep and long cut; an incision of considerable length and depth, particularly in flesh.
